==Translingual==

===Han character===
{{Han char|rn=86|rad=火|as=12|sn=16|four=93833|canj=FBKF|ids=⿰火然}}

====References====
{{Han ref|kx=0682.070|dkj=19394|dj=1094.060|hdz=32235.110|uh=71C3}}

==Chinese==

===Glyph origin===
{{Han etym}}
{{Han compound|火|然|ls=psc|c1=s|c2=p|t1=fire}} – burn with fire. Originally {{och-l|然}}, but because it was phonetically borrowed for abstract senses, {{m|zh|火}} was added for differentiation.

===Etymology===
Probably {{der|zh|sit}}; compare {{cog|kac|nan||to light}}, {{m|kac|shanan||torch}} ({{zh-ref|Schuessler, 2007}}).

====Definitions====
{{head|zh|hanzi}}

# to [[burn]]; to be [[alight]]
# to [[ignite]]; to [[light]]
# to [[spark]] off (hopes); to [[start]] (debate); to [[raise]] (hopes)
# {{lb|zh|neologism|slang}} [[lit]]; [[awesome]]; [[cool]]; [[uplifting]]
# {{lb|zh|neologism|slang}} [[pumped up]]; [[hyper]]; [[stoked]]

=====Synonyms=====
* {{s|to burn}} {{zh-l|焚燒}}
* {{s|to ignite}}
{{syn-saurus|zh|點燃}}
